info.title: Convinced Campaign API
info.description: Programmatic access to the Convinced campaign system. Create campaigns, push target-company intel, and resolve widget tokens for personalized outreach. The widget loads with `?c=<token>` and grounds the conversation in the intel you supply (or that Convinced synthesizes for you).

post.description: Campaigns group target-company rows under a stable slug. Calling this endpoint with an existing slug updates the campaign's name or description; the slug itself is immutable.

post.summary: Create or update a campaign target
post.description:
Upsert a target company in a campaign. Two modes are supported:

- **Partner-intel mode (`enrich: false`):** supply a fully-formed `intel` object. The row is widget-ready as soon as the request returns, with `enrichmentStatus: "skipped"`.
- **Auto-enrich mode (`enrich: true`):** supply `rawResearch` (plain text or markdown) and optional `links`. Convinced runs the AI research pipeline asynchronously; poll the GET endpoint until `enrichmentStatus` flips from `pending` → `running` → `done` (typically 30-90s).

The `token` becomes the `?c=<token>` query string on the widget URL. Tokens are unique per organization.

partnerIntel.description: Use when you already have structured account intel from your CRM or research team.

intel.companySummary: Mid-market 3PL focused on last-mile dispatch for grocery and pharmacy.

champion.name: Priya Rao
champion.context: Owns dispatch SLA and fleet utilization KPIs.

painPoints: Dispatch decisions are manual and slow during peak windows.
No single source of truth across TMS, OMS, and routing.

value.additionalContext: Lead with ROI on dispatch speed.

autoEnrich.description: Send rep notes + landing-page URLs; Convinced synthesizes the intel object.

value.rawResearch:
## Account notes
- Mid-market 3PL, last-mile dispatch for grocery + pharmacy.
- ~300 drivers, expanding into Tier-2.
- Champion: Priya Rao, VP Ops. Cares about dispatch SLA + fleet utilization.
- Currently on SAP TMS + Locus, evaluating FarEye for visibility.
value.additionalContext: Lead with ROI on dispatch speed; this account misses peak-window SLAs.

409.description: Token is already used by another target in this organization.

get.description: Returns the current target state, including enrichment lifecycle status. Poll this endpoint after an auto-enrich upsert until `enrichmentStatus` is `done` (or `failed`, with `enrichmentError` populated).

404.description: Target not found in this campaign.

bearerAuth.description: Partner API key issued from the Convinced dashboard. API keys are organization-bound — never share a key across organizations.

slug.description: Stable campaign identifier. Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens. If omitted, derived from `name`. Maximum 64 chars.

status.description: Only campaigns with `status: "active"` resolve targets at the widget.

TargetUpsertRequest.description: Exactly one of `intel` (with `enrich: false`) or `rawResearch` (with `enrich: true` or omitted) must be present.

companyName.description: Target account or company name. Used in the agent's opening message.

token.description: Stable target identifier passed to the widget as `?c=<token>`. Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens; max 64 chars. If omitted, derived from `companyName`.

enrich.description: When `true` (default), Convinced synthesizes the `intel` object from `rawResearch` + `links` asynchronously. When `false`, the partner-supplied `intel` object is used as-is.

intel.description: Required when `enrich: false`. See the Intel schema for the exact shape.

rawResearch.description: Rep notes, CRM excerpts, account research. Plain text or markdown. Used by the auto-enrich pipeline as the primary source of truth. Recommend keeping under 20-30k chars.
rawResearch.example:
## Account notes
- Mid-market 3PL, last-mile dispatch.
- ~300 drivers, expanding into Tier-2.
- Champion: Priya Rao, VP Ops.

links.description: URLs the auto-enrich pipeline will crawl for additional context. Microsite landing pages, news articles, account pages.

additionalContext.description: Strategic instructions for how the agent should frame this account. Not synthesized — appended verbatim to the agent prompt.
additionalContext.example: Lead with ROI on dispatch speed.

CampaignIntel.description: Structured target intel. Every field maps to a specific agent behavior — partners cannot add arbitrary fields.

companySummary.description: One or two factual sentences describing what the prospect company actually does. The agent mirrors this back early in the conversation.
companySummary.example: Mid-market 3PL focused on last-mile dispatch for grocery and pharmacy chains across India.

painPoints.description: Concrete problems the prospect is dealing with, ranked most-to-least relevant. Phrase as the BUYER would describe the pain — not your product or solution. Drives the discovery flow and on-screen suggestion pills. Recommend 3-6 entries.
example: Dispatch decisions are manual and slow during peak windows.

industry.description: Industry or vertical. Used for case-study selection and to calibrate vocabulary.

size.description: Free-form headcount, revenue band, or stage. Calibrates SMB vs. mid-market vs. enterprise positioning.

champion.description: The primary buyer at the account — the persona the agent imagines it is talking to.

context.description: One-line background: priorities, tenure, owned KPIs.
context.example: Owns dispatch SLA and fleet utilization KPIs.

currentStack.description: Tools the prospect ALREADY USES in adjacent or related categories — broader stack, not direct competitors. The agent uses this to talk integrations and avoid suggesting things they have.

recentSignals.description: Recent news or triggers the agent can reference: funding, leadership, launches, expansions.
example: Expanding into Tier-2 cities per Oct earnings call

competitorUsage.description: A SPECIFIC direct competitor the prospect uses today or is evaluating — i.e. someone who does what you do. Sets up switch arguments and head-to-head positioning.
competitorUsage.example: Datadog (incumbent since 2022, evaluating switch due to cost).

relevantCaseStudies.description: Customer names you want the agent to bring up as proof points. Validated against your org's referenceable case studies. Unknown names are dropped silently and surfaced in `response.warnings.droppedCaseStudies`. Match is case-insensitive.

enrichedAt.description: Set when synthesis completed (auto-enrich) or at insertion (partner-intel).

widgetToken.description: The token to pass into the widget.

widgetParam.description: The query parameter name the widget reads from.

warnings.description: Present when one or more `relevantCaseStudies` names were dropped because they don't match referenceable customers.

enrichmentStartedAt.description: Set when enrichment began. Useful for detecting stuck jobs.

hasIntel.description: True when the `intel` object is present on the row.

widgetReady.description: True when the campaign is active, intel is present, and `enrichmentStatus` is `done` or `skipped`. Send the visitor to the widget once this flips to `true`.

EnrichmentStatus.description: Lifecycle state of the auto-enrich pipeline. `pending` (queued), `running` (synthesis in flight), `done` (intel ready), `failed` (synthesis errored), `skipped` (partner-intel mode, no synthesis needed).
